title: Microsoft preps 'Project Perception,' a multi-model rival to Anthropic Mythos
subtitle: The tool routes vulnerability-hunting queries across Microsoft, OpenAI and Anthropic models to undercut Mythos on price.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: The Information broke a July 17 report — since picked up by TechRepublic and Neowin — that Microsoft is finalizing a security product called Project Perception, expected to launch this month. Perception scans code for vulnerabilities and drafts patches, using a model router that assigns each query to a Microsoft, OpenAI or Anthropic model depending on which is cheapest and strongest for the task. It is being positioned directly against Anthropic's Mythos, whose API pricing runs materially higher than Opus-class models.

The mechanism matters more than the branding. Perception is one of the first serious enterprise products to make model choice invisible to the buyer. Customers pay for a security outcome; Microsoft picks which lab's model runs under the hood, per query. If it works, it commoditizes the 'pick a foundation model' step that has dominated AI procurement for two years, and turns the router itself into the moat.

This lands into a market where Anthropic's Mythos has been the reference tool for AI-native vulnerability research since spring — it is the model that helped find the Linux GhostLock privilege escalation bug that hid for 15 years. Microsoft is not claiming a smarter model; it is claiming a smarter dispatch layer. If enterprise buyers accept that framing, every frontier lab loses direct pricing power inside the fastest-growing enterprise AI category.

For learners: 'which model is best' is becoming the wrong question. The interesting engineering question is now 'which model should I call for this specific task at this specific price ceiling', and that is a routing problem, not a benchmarking one. If you are building an AI product, treat model selection as a runtime decision, not a design-time one — and expect the router to be the thing that ships value, not the model behind it.

sources.title: Microsoft's 'Project Perception' Could Challenge Anthropic's Mythos in AI Security — TechRepublic
Microsoft to launch Project Perception AI cybersecurity platform this month — NewsBytes
Recent patches for Windows 11 could have been created by Microsoft's new Mythos rival — Neowin
